items.prompt: Which three barriers recur in Section 13.1.3?
choices: Inaccessible datasets, undocumented methods, and lack of standards
Only CAP theorem, sharding, and quorum writes
Only GAN mode collapse, FID, and inception score
Only slide fonts, Marp themes, and PDF export
items.explain: The chapter lists inaccessible datasets, undocumented methods, and lack of standards as recurring barriers.

items.prompt: HIPAA-style privacy limits mean clinical data can never be shared in any form for open science.
items.explain: Open science pairs sharing goals with de-identification and anonymization so data can be released safely when allowed.

items.prompt: Missing hyperparameters and preprocessing steps mainly illustrate which barrier?
choices: Undocumented methods that block faithful replication
items.explain: Example 13.6 shows that omitting hyperparameters and preprocessing blocks replication.

items.prompt: Why do incompatible gene-naming conventions across labs matter for reproducibility?
items.rubric: Accept answers about integration/comparison difficulty, interoperability, or inability to combine multi-study datasets.
items.sample_answer: Different naming standards make it hard to integrate and compare datasets across studies.

items.prompt: Reproducibility standards matter in part because high-stakes decisions, such as drug development, depend on trustworthy trial results.
items.explain: Example 13.8 links unreproducible clinical trial results to compromised drug approval decisions.
